name: Docker Baseline
description: Prevents high-risk Docker operations: privileged container runs, host filesystem mounts, system pruning, and unapproved image pushes. Blocks operations that can escape container isolation or cause irreversible data loss.

rules.name: Block mounting host root filesystem
rules.description: docker run -v /: mounts the host root into the container, granting full read/write access to the host OS. This is a critical security boundary violation.

rules.name: Block privileged container runs
rules.description: The --privileged flag disables all container isolation. Privileged containers can access host devices and modify kernel parameters.

rules.name: Block docker system prune -a
rules.description: docker system prune -a and docker volume prune remove all unused images, containers, networks, and volumes. This causes irreversible data loss.

rules.name: Require access request to push images to a registry
rules.description: Pushing images to a container registry publishes potentially sensitive code or credentials. Requires admin approval.

rules.name: Require justification for force-removing containers
rules.description: docker rm -f forcibly stops and removes running containers, potentially losing in-memory state or unsaved data.

rules.name: Require justification for docker exec
rules.description: Executing commands directly inside a running container allows arbitrary code execution in the container's environment.
